seoTitle: Canterbury vs Canton Beach — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Canterbury and Canton Beach. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Canterbury vs Canton Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Canterbury and Canton Beach.
heading: Canterbury vs Canton Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Canterbury, New South Wales and Canton Beach, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Canton Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Canterbury (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Canterbury or Canton Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Canterbury has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Canton Beach scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Canterbury scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Canton Beach scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Canterbury or Canton Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Canton Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
